note: the design doc's headline spot-check. Invariant (not exact-top) because 'lemon' vs 'lemon-cake' compete closely for rank 1 on token overlap — both are correct, reachable hits; pinning membership + a sane score floor is the load-bearing behavior, not which food-node wins the top slot.
the Purple-Wedding-feast spot-check — this exact phrase appears in the book text (Sansa's POV) and should rank the event node unambiguously first, with a navigable book cite.
the guest-right custom node itself, not just a mention (e.g. walder-frey), must rank first — a query naming a concept should surface the concept's own node before an incidental quote referencing it.
the --type/opts.type filter must exclude non-food hits (e.g. colemon, a character node whose quote happens to mention cakes) that would otherwise rank in an unfiltered search.
no token in the query appears in any doc's postings -> empty result, not an error.
whitespace-only query has zero tokens after tokenization -> [] (trust-boundary convention, mirrors resolve()'s cleanPhrase — no exception on bad input).
